steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims snowball sampling may be superior to purposive sampling for recruiting participants with the desired characteristics. Supporting evidence must show snowball sampling outperforming purposive sampling at that specific task.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Most participants recruited through snowball sampling had different characteristics than the initial participants who recruited them.: Different characteristics from the initial participants would not show that snowball sampling recruited more participants with the desired characteristics than purposive sampling did. ✗
  • Choice B — Participants recruited through snowball sampling were more likely to have taken part in previous studies than were participants recruited through purposive sampling.: Prior study participation is not the relevant outcome; the conclusion concerns recruiting people with the desired characteristics. ✗
  • Choice C — Snowball sampling yielded significantly more participants with the desired characteristics than purposive sampling did.: A direct head-to-head comparison showing snowball recruits more qualifying participants — exactly what 'superior' means here. ✓
  • Choice D — Snowball sampling yielded significantly more participants than would be expected if researchers contacted people without regard to those people's characteristics.: Does not compare the methods on the relevant outcome. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
